Waving is a universal greeting across many cultures
Wave your hand gently to say "Hello".
Sign languages have full grammar like spoken languages
Touch chin → move hand forward to say "Thank You".
Head nods are common signs of agreement worldwide
Make a fist and nod it up and down to say "Yes".
Body language often communicates faster than words
Shake index and middle finger to say "No".
One palm hits the other to say "Stop".
Asking for help improves teamwork and learning
One hand lifts the other upward to say "Help".
Many food gestures are similar across cultures
Bring fingers to mouth like you are eating.
Gesture-based communication is used even by babies
Pretend to hold a cup and sip to say "Drink".
Friendship gestures exist in many sign languages
Hook two fingers together to say "Friend".
Emotional gestures are easy to recognize globally
Cross arms over chest to say "Love".
Listen carefully. What animal is this?
A dog says "Woof". Tap the dog!
Cats use different meows to communicate
A cat says "Meow". Tap the cat!
Birds sing to communicate and attract mates
A bird says "Chirp". Tap the bird!
Listen carefully. What is this sound?
Rain sounds can help people relax
Rain falls from the clouds. Tap the rain!
Horns are used for safety warnings
A car horn goes "Beep". Tap the car!
Bells are used in schools and temples
A bell rings "Ding Dong". Tap the bell!
Ocean waves crash on the shore. Tap the ocean!
A keyboard makes a "Click" sound. Tap the keyboard!
Humans can recognize people by footsteps
Footsteps make a "Thump" sound. Tap the footsteps!
Listen carefully. Can you hear two sounds?
The brain can separate multiple sounds at once
Listen for the dog and the bell. Tap the correct mix!
C-A-T spells CAT. Tap the letters!
This is a circle. It is perfectly round.
A circle has no corners. Tap the circle!
D-O-G spells DOG. Tap the letters!
This is a triangle. It has three sides.
Triangles are strong shapes used in structures
A triangle has three corners. Tap the triangle!
The sun is the main energy source for Earth
S-U-N spells SUN. Tap the letters!
This is a square. It has four equal sides.
Squares are used in buildings and designs
A square has four corners. Tap the square!
B-A-L-L spells BALL. Tap the letters!
This is a star. It has many points.
Stars are massive balls of gas in space
A star shines bright. Tap the star!
T-R-E-E spells TREE. Tap the letters!
Can you find a water bottle? Show it to the camera!
Water bottles help us stay hydrated while we play!
Pick up a bottle and show it to the camera.
The first books were written on clay tablets thousands of years ago!
Hold a book up to the camera.
Can you find a mobile phone? We use them to talk to friends.
Mobile phones are actually powerful computers that fit in your pocket!
Show a phone to the camera.
Is there a chair nearby? Stand next to it or show it!
The oldest chairs in the world are over 4,500 years old!
Point the camera at a chair.
A single pencil can draw a line that is 35 miles long!
Show a pen or pencil to the camera.
